title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
input_row.description: Disable anonymous requests to the API server.
input_row.rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests. These requests are then served by the API server. You should rely on authentication to authorize access and disallow anonymous requests. If you are using RBAC authorization, it is generally considered reasonable to allow anonymous access to the API Server for health checks and discovery purposes, and hence this recommendation is not scored. However, you should consider whether anonymous discovery is an acceptable risk for your purposes.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false . Alternative Audit kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command} {"\n"}{end}' | grep '\--anonymous- auth' | grep -i false If the exit code is '1', then the control isn't present / failed
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the below parameter. --anonymous-auth=false
input_row.default_value: By default, anonymous access is enabled. Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using `kubectl` commands to check the `--anonymous-auth` argument, which indicates that this property can be queried via Kubernetes APIs.
- The control is about a standard Kubernetes service property (API server configuration).
- The `kubectl` command provided in the audit section can be executed to verify the configuration without requiring SSH or manual inspection.
- Therefore, the control can be evaluated automatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_anonymous_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the `kubectl` command: 
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod -nkube-system -lcomponent=kube-apiserver -o=jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.spec.containers[*].command} {{"\n"}}{end}' | grep '\--anonymous-auth' | grep -i false
  ```
- This command retrieves the command line arguments of the kube-apiserver pods in the kube-system namespace.
- It checks for the presence of the `--anonymous-auth` argument and verifies that it is set to `false`.
- **Pass Criteria:** The command should return the line containing `--anonymous-auth=false`.
- **Fail Criteria:** If the command returns no output or the argument is not set to `false`, the control is considered failed.
